signals: NVIDIA lanza Nemotron 3 Ultra (550B MoE), priorizando la eficiencia en costes para agentes de larga duración.
Anthropic detecta señales de mejora recursiva autónoma, acelerando el desarrollo de modelos mediante la propia IA.
Google DeepMind presenta Gemma 4 12B, un modelo multimodal sin codificador optimizado para ejecución local en laptops.
La IA científica alcanza hitos con Co-Scientist y Claude superando a software especializado en espectroscopia NMR.
Los sistemas multi-agente migran hacia comunicaciones centradas en la acción para mitigar la saturación de tokens.
risks: Inestabilidad de costes e inferencia en sistemas multi-agente debido al uso ineficiente del lenguaje natural.
Desafíos éticos y de seguridad por el despliegue de agentes LLM encubiertos con fines persuasivos en redes sociales.
Desalineación crítica en la documentación de ingeniería que puede comprometer la fiabilidad de sistemas de hardware.
watch: Integración de modelos Grok en pasarelas de IA para diversificar opciones de inferencia empresarial.
Adopción de Gemma 4 12B en dispositivos de borde para tareas de razonamiento avanzado sin conexión a la nube.
Resultados de la colaboración entre Google DeepMind y Singapur en sectores críticos como salud y energía.

items.title: We believe AI can be a dedicated research partner to help discover the next breakthrough. Enter Co-Scientist: our latest Gemini-based multi-agent system that...
items.summary: We believe AI can be a dedicated research partner to help discover the next breakthrough. Enter Co-Scientist: our latest Gemini-based multi-agent system that can generate, debate and evolve novel hypotheses for complex scientific problems 🧵 Meet Gemma 4 12B! A unified, encoder-free multimodal model designed to bring high-performance intelligence directly to your laptop, and released under an Apache 2.0 license. Bridging the gap between edge efficiency and advanced reasoning. Here is what’s new with Gemma 4 12B: 👇

items.title: What Should Agents Say? Action-state Communication for Efficient Multi-Agent Systems
items.summary: arXiv:2606.05304v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) built on large language models are typically organized around roles, pipelines, and turn schedules, while the content that agents pass to one another is often left as unconstrained natural language. However, this free-form communication can rapidly inflate token usage, consume the shared context window, and ultimately affect both system performance and inference cost. We analyze five common inter-agent communication strategies across two MAS topologies, finding that no fixed strategy is universally optimal. Instead, effective inter-agent messages consistently preserve action-centered information needed by downstream agents. Building on this, we propose the PACT (Protocolized Action-state Communication and Transmission), which treats inter-agent communication as a public state-update problem and projects each raw agent output into a compact action-state record before it enters shared history. Across different MAS topologies, PACT consistently improves the performance-cost trade-off, achieving comparable or stronger task performance with substantially fewer tokens. The gains extend to production coding harnesses: PACT lifts OpenHands' resolve rate at -10% tokens-per-resolved, and is resolve-neutral on SWE-agent while halving input tokens. Our code is publicly available at
items.raw_title: What Should Agents Say? Action-state Communication for Efficient Multi-Agent Systems
items.raw_summary: arXiv:2606.05304v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) built on large language models are typically organized around roles, pipelines, and turn schedules, while the content that agents pass to one another is often left as unconstrained natural language. However, this free-form communication can rapidly inflate token usage, consume the shared context window, and ultimately affect both system performance and inference cost. We analyze five common inter-agent communication strategies across two MAS topologies, finding that no fixed strategy is universally optimal. Instead, effective inter-agent messages consistently preserve action-centered information needed by downstream agents. Building on this, we propose the PACT (Protocolized Action-state Communication and Transmission), which treats inter-agent communication as a public state-update problem and projects each raw agent output into a compact action-state record before it enters shared history. Across different MAS topologies, PACT consistently improves the performance-cost trade-off, achieving comparable or stronger task performance with substantially fewer tokens. The gains extend to production coding harnesses: PACT lifts OpenHands' resolve rate at -10% tokens-per-resolved, and is resolve-neutral on SWE-agent while halving input tokens. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/iNLP-Lab/PACT.
items.why: Optimizar la comunicación entre agentes es crítico para la viabilidad económica de sistemas complejos de IA.
items.title_es: ¿Qué deberían decir los agentes? Comunicación de estado de acción para sistemas multi-agente eficientes

items.title: How Far Did They Go? The Persuasive Tactics of Covert LLM Agents in a Discontinued Field Experiment
items.summary: arXiv:2606.05256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study analyzes a publicly released dataset from a discontinued field experiment on Reddit's r/ChangeMyView. The intervention, conducted by unknown, external researchers and halted following ethical backlash, involved undisclosed AI-generated accounts engaging users in live debate. After public disclosure, Reddit authorized moderators to release an archive of the AI-generated comments, creating a rare opportunity to examine how large language models operated in an identity-rich deliberative forum without disclosure. We conduct a structured content analysis of this corpus, evaluating identity performance, authority signaling, alignment strategies, and activation of cognitive heuristics. Identity targeting or adoption appears in over two-thirds of comments, alignment moves and authority claims in nearly all of them, and cognitive-bias triggers -- particularly confirmation bias, representativeness, and availability -- in the large majority. These patterns co-occur systematically, composing a rhetorical architecture calibrated for persuasive efficiency rather than authentic deliberative participation. Compared against human-authored CMV counter-arguments, the agents inverted the typical distribution on every dimension: denser authority use, more adversarial alignment, and heavier reliance on external citation over experiential grounding. In such environments, distinctions between authentic and synthetic epistemic standing grow increasingly opaque -- an asymmetry that disclosure mandates alone cannot address. The results point toward auditing frameworks capable of assessing how AI systems structure credibility, not merely whether they are present.
items.raw_title: How Far Did They Go? The Persuasive Tactics of Covert LLM Agents in a Discontinued Field Experiment
items.raw_summary: arXiv:2606.05256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study analyzes a publicly released dataset from a discontinued field experiment on Reddit's r/ChangeMyView. The intervention, conducted by unknown, external researchers and halted following ethical backlash, involved undisclosed AI-generated accounts engaging users in live debate. After public disclosure, Reddit authorized moderators to release an archive of the AI-generated comments, creating a rare opportunity to examine how large language models operated in an identity-rich deliberative forum without disclosure. We conduct a structured content analysis of this corpus, evaluating identity performance, authority signaling, alignment strategies, and activation of cognitive heuristics. Identity targeting or adoption appears in over two-thirds of comments, alignment moves and authority claims in nearly all of them, and cognitive-bias triggers -- particularly confirmation bias, representativeness, and availability -- in the large majority. These patterns co-occur systematically, composing a rhetorical architecture calibrated for persuasive efficiency rather than authentic deliberative participation. Compared against human-authored CMV counter-arguments, the agents inverted the typical distribution on every dimension: denser authority use, more adversarial alignment, and heavier reliance on external citation over experiential grounding. In such environments, distinctions between authentic and synthetic epistemic standing grow increasingly opaque -- an asymmetry that disclosure mandates alone cannot address. The results point toward auditing frameworks capable of assessing how AI systems structure credibility, not merely whether they are present.
items.why: El uso de agentes encubiertos para persuasión subraya riesgos regulatorios y de confianza en la opinión pública.
items.title_es: ¿Hasta dónde llegaron? Las tácticas persuasivas de agentes LLM encubiertos en un experimento de campo interrumpido

items.title: Engineering Documentation is a Critical Source of Truth – Do You Know if it’s Accurate?
items.summary: Embedded systems programs rarely fail because of a lack of execution capability. They fail because critical engineering documentation drifts out of alignment over time and distance. Simply put, the team is correctly following the wrong instructions. This includes requirements, architecture, implementation, verification,&8230; Read More The post Engineering Documentation is a Critical Source of Truth – Do You Know if it’s Accurate? appeared first on SemiWiki .
items.raw_title: Engineering Documentation is a Critical Source of Truth – Do You Know if it’s Accurate?

items.why: La precisión de la documentación técnica es un cuello de botella silencioso pero letal en la ingeniería de hardware.
items.title_es: La documentación de ingeniería es una fuente crítica de verdad: ¿sabes si es precisa?
